snippets.title: Manual chain rule for sin(x²)
snippets.explanation: Outer cos(u) evaluated at u=x² times inner derivative 2x implements (f∘g)′(x) by hand.

snippets.title: Backprop through a two-layer net in PyTorch
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

torch.manual_seed(0)
model = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4, 3), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(3, 1))
x = torch.randn(2, 4)
y = torch.randn(2, 1)
loss = ((model(x) - y) ** 2).mean()
loss.backward()
print(model[0].weight.grad.shape)  # dL/dW for first layer
snippets.explanation: `backward()` applies reverse-mode chain rule on the recorded graph, populating `.grad` for every parameter.

snippets.title: Verify chain rule with gradcheck
snippets.explanation: Autograd's result should match the manual chain rule product cos(x²)·2x.
